programme.title: A Monster Calls (2016)
programme.desc: Young Conor struggles to deal with his mother's terminal illness, the cold grandmother he has to stay with and the bullying he receives at school. He is woken from a recurring nightmare by the visitation of a tree monster at his bedroom window, telling him a series of stories. In return, Conor must tell the monster the truth behind his dreadful nightmare. programme.desc: Daily advice for viewers in self-isolation due to the Covid-19 outbreak(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a Birmingham property with a tiny kitchen, a former care home near Conwy and a house in Merseyside(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers who toil through the small hours to get the world ready for when the rest of the population awakes. In the first edition, vets perform potentially life-saving surgery on a much-loved horse, and bus cleaners work to have Newcastle's transport ready for commuters(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help teams to buy items to make a profit at auction. Natasha also visits the National Tramway Museum in Crich to learn about the role trams played in delivering the nation's letters(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Izzie arrives ahead of schedule, putting Daniel's plans in disarray. Zara goes to see Emma, while Jasmine gets irritated at work, and Ruhma finds herself caught in the middle(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man is murdered at the unveiling of a new church clock and the cleric must act quickly before an innocent woman is sent to the gallows. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton leads a couple in their search for a countryside home in the border between Staffordshire and Derbyshire border on a budget of £320,000. Later, Alistair puts his tailoring skills to the test when he visits the manufacturing factory of heritage knitwear brand John Smedley(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling expert EJ Osborne is at Woking Recycling Centre searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp, including an old bedroom cabinet and a box of wooden blocks(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, restoring a set of grocer's scales, a leather keepsake bag and an ornamental Egyptian table(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: More than one and a half million people have been told they have to stay at home for the next three months at least because they are most at risk from coronavirus, with the Government promising to do whatever it takes to support them. Richard Bilton hears from some of the most vulnerable about how their lives have changed and the people trying to help them(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian takes great pleasure in kicking Dotty out but he may regret his actions when she realises what he has done and plans her next move. A drunken Billy helps Linda and Mick put their problems into perspective, Chantelle throws herself into work with the Panesars, and Bobby develops a crush on Dotty(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand, singer Emma Bunton, athlete Jonnie Peacock and actor Shaun Williamson join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals begin with a spectacular challenge as the nine remaining contestants head to the Royal Navy base in Portsmouth, where they have to cook for HMS Diamond's Trafalgar Night celebration dinner. Split into three teams, they face the enormous challenge of mass catering at fine-dining level from the ship's galley. They then return to the MasterChef kitchen, where they must create a dish to hold onto their hard-fought place in the competition, with two being sent home at the end by judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace(n)

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: While trying to do a good deed by helping out at the local food bank, Mobeen is also under strict orders from Uncle Khan to launder money - and juggling the two things could leave him with a deadly debt that's impossible to pay(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with actor and comedian Miles Jupp and journalist Helen Lewis joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: World darts champion Peter Wright, Paralympic great Sarah Storey, former Premier League striker Dion Dublin and ex-England cricketer Steve Harmison join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Featuring a group of dogs riding Russia's underground rail system, and a clever crow that can use a pedestrian crossing to crack a tough problem(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: 11-year-old Holly needs a relaxing den to help her forget about her actinic dermatitis(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends go on a road trip across the UK(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall takes a boat ride up the Amazon to come face to face with a black piranha. He also encounters electric eels, meets a maned wolf and braves crocodile-infested waters(n)

programme.title: Astronauts: Do You Have What It Takes?
programme.desc: Twelve people compete against each other through six weeks of astronaut training, with the winner receiving support in pursuing a career with a space agency. Former astronaut Chris Hadfield, medical researcher Dr Kevin Fong and psychologist Dr Iya Whiteley oversee the challenges, and can eliminate contenders from the show at any time. In the first episode, they must learn to hover a helicopter(n)

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Secret of the Wings (2012)
programme.desc: The fairy becomes curious about the mysterious winter forest where fairies never dare to go. She travels into the cold woods, where she meets a new friend and uncovers a threat to both their homes that they must work together to prevent. Disney animated adventure, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Lucy Hale, Anjelica Huston and Timothy Dalton(n)

programme.title: Birds of Paradise: The Ultimate Quest
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thirty years ago explorer Benedict Allen lived in Papua New Guinea with the Niowra, a remote people. Broadcast journalist Frank Gardner has always wished to see wild birds of paradise, so Benedict resolves to take him along. The duo set out through some of the most inhospitable terrain on the planet, negotiating swamps, mountains and crocodile-infested waters, heading into the cloud forest. Despite Frank requiring the use of a wheelchair following a shooting incident in 2004, Benedict determines to get him to their destination(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: As the competition continues, chefs Gordon Jones, Roberta Hall, Ross Bryans and Amy Elles compete in the heats for Scotland. Each chef prepares a starter and fish course celebrating iconic food from children's literature, including a venison tartare with smoked celeriac remoulade which pays homage to J K Rowling's Harry Potter franchise, and a Wind in the Willows inspired dish featuring a sourdough baguette and chicken terrine(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Scottish chefs are at the halfway point of their heat, serving up their mains and desserts celebrating children's literature. One of the contestants attempts an ambitious nose-to-tail rabbit dish as a homage to Toad's feasting in The Wind in the Willows, while another takes inspiration from Dundee's comic hero Desperate Dan and tries to replicate the cowboy's famous cow pie using Scottish beef fillet, beef cheeks, bone marrow rissole and puff pastry. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: Tricks of the Trade
programme.desc: Piers Taylor helps a family build an extension on their ex-council house in Warwick, suggesting that they ditch their ambitious plan and try a simpler one. He designs clever storage, helps them find a low-cost solution for the floor, and creates a multi-coloured porch that would normally cost thousands. Meanwhile, Cheryl Pilliner-Reeves comes to the aid of Karen and David in Ipswich, who are desperate to fall in love with their Victorian terrace, which has a mix of levels and corridors. She must find a way to connect it all, and creates two bespoke staircases for just £600, as well as helping Karen get her dream kitchen(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall's tour of the West Country continues as he visits the Dorset-Devon border - his home territory. Here, he investigates efforts to bring beavers back to the region for the first time since the 16th century. At Abbotsbury Swannery, resident swan herd Dave Wheeler rounds up his charges for a routine health check, and bat enthusiast Colin Morris offers Hugh an insight into why he transformed a former mansion into a plush sanctuary for Dorset's greater horseshoe bats(n)

programme.title: The Muppets (2011)
programme.desc: Three fans of the famous puppet variety show discover a powerful businessman is going to knock down the Muppet theatre and drill for oil on the site. They track down frontman Kermit the frog and persuade him to reunite his co-stars, so they can put on one last performance to save the venue. Comedy, starring Jason Segel, Amy Adams and Chris Cooper, with songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at TV appearances by stars of the silver screen from the 1970s, using archive footage to tell the story of their lives and careers(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon takes part in an exercise with the regular army and everything goes well - until pompous Captain Mainwaring assumes the role of an enemy agent on the run. Comedy, starring Arthur Lowe and Clive Dunn. First shown in 1974(n)

programme.title: Australia: Earth's Magical Kingdom
programme.desc: An exploration of how native creatures have adapted to survive in the human environment. In Melbourne, tree-dwelling possums have taken to sharing trees in city parks, risking encounters with traffic and cats. In Brisbane's parks, over the past 150 years of so, eastern water dragons have become twice the size of their wild cousins and without trees to climb their limbs are getting longer and claws shorter. Beyond the cities, dingoes eek out an existence in the inhospitable environment of a desert goldmine, while climate change and deforestation is posing a threat to koalas. Narrated by Barry Humphries(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a new quest as he travels around South-east Asia, guided by his 1913 Bradshaw's Handbook on a 2,500-mile railway adventure across six countries. Beginning in Hong Kong, the former politician investigates how Britain won the island and Kowloon from China after two mid-19th century wars over the trade in opium. He also boards the island's most famous funicular to the Peak and visits the state-of-the art West Kowloon station with its high-speed link to China - the largest underground high-speed station in the world(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig looks at 'Quantity' and 'Quality', inviting Bridget Christie, Joe Lycett and James Acaster to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting(n)

programme.title: Titian - Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: The story behind the artist's commission for Philip II of Spain, known as the poesie paintings, which had not been seen together for over 450 years and had been the subject of an exhibition at London's National Gallery before it was closed due to the Covid-19 outbreak. Cameras were recently granted access to bring this groundbreaking exhibition to the screens across Britain and beyond(n)

programme.title: Ghost Stories (2017)
programme.desc: Professor Philip Goodman, famous for debunking hoaxes and ghost sightings, is given a dossier of three terrifying, unsolvable cases by his hero and role model Charles Cameron, who has been missing, presumed dead, for years. Can Goodman explain what even Cameron could not? Horror anthology, starring Andy Nyman, Martin Freeman and Paul Whitehouse(n)

programme.title: Timbuktu (2014)
programme.desc: A cattle herder living a peaceful life in the desert with his wife and children accidentally crosses paths with the Jihadists who control the nearby ancient town of Timbuktu. Overnight, his world is shattered, and everything he loves and values is threatened. Drama, starring Ibrahim Ahmed, Abel Jafri and Toulou Kiki. In French, Arabic, Bambara, English and Songhay(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke look to China for inspiration as they tackle a rubbish-strewn back garden in Lochwinnoch, Scotland(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode continues his alphabetical tour through the BBC's culinary archive, this time selecting recipes and ingredients linked by the letter D. Raymond Blanc cooks roast duck with celeriac puree and a blackberry sauce, and Gregg Wallace serves up potato dauphinoise(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The programme joins Adam Frost in his garden to bring viewers inspiration for their plots, along with all the essential gardening jobs for this weekend. Plus, the team presents a guide through seasonal highlights from across the country, visiting stunning gardens, meeting gardeners and finding out their secrets of success.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison visits the Lugg Valley in Herefordshire to hunt for signs of spring, joining volunteers making sure toads can make it safely across main roads to their favoured spawning grounds. She also meets people who have just planted out their first forest garden and chats to an artist who sees the beauty in the flooded landscape(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: The stories of two remarkable groups who grow by the community, for the community - a vegetable growing initiative in Dunkeld and a village square in Easthaven(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Host Susan Calman invites the two highest-scoring chefs from Scotland to go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. The duo need to impress formidable judges Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver as well as guest judge David Baddiel, the bestselling children's author and comedian. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Short
programme.desc: The food enthusiasts take their longest trip yet, searching for pies and pasties. They begin their journey in Cornwall, heading to the Midlands to visit an orchard and produce an apple pie, and northwards to Melton Mowbray, where Si King makes chicken and mushroom pie. The duo's trip ends in Scotland, where they cook salmon coulibiac for Highland dancers(n)

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: Exploring the forests of the Yucatan Peninsula, which contain the ruins of the Mayan civilisation and a wide range of wildlife, including jaguars, monkeys and tropical birds. The region also holds a secret that is the key to how Mexico's people and animals are able to survive during the country's dry season(n)

programme.title: Rugby League's Legendary Watersplash Final
programme.desc: Dave Woods presents a look back at the 1968 Challenge Cup final between Leeds and Wakefield Trinity at Wembley Stadium. The match is best remembered for Don Fox's incredible conversion miss in the dying minutes, which handed Leeds victory by an 11-10 scoreline, but also for the waterlogged state of the pitch. Dave speaks to surviving members of both teams, including Don's brother Neil, who talks about the impact of the fateful kick on his sibling's life and career(n)

programme.title: Topkapi (1964)
programme.desc: International thieves mastermind the intricate robbery of an emerald dagger from Istanbul's Topkapi Palace Museum, and hire a small-time conman to drive the getaway car. Unfortunately, when the police find firearms in his vehicle, they coerce the hapless driver into spying for them. Crime comedy, starring Peter Ustinov in an Oscar-winning performance, with Melina Mercouri, Maximilian Schell, Robert Morley and Akim Tamiroff(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his search through previously classified documents in the National Archives, learning how Mary Whitehouse defended God in a court of law. He also discloses details of the last woman to be imprisoned under the Witchcraft Acts, and unearths files that reveal the Church's role in slavery(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Piccadilly Circus to Gravesend, visiting an exotic West End store that was popular among Victorian women and hearing how a Russian ballet company took London by storm in 1909. He also discovers the origins of netball and gymslips at North Kent College and meets the pilots of the Port of London Authority, founded in the early 20th century(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns reflect on the lessons learned through the series, and how corner shops can continue to thrive in the changing world of 21st-century business. The programme examines how shops have reinvented themselves to compete with mini-supermarkets, the rise of zero-waste shops, and how other businesses, like cafes, coffee shops and micro pubs, are filling the role of community hub(n)

programme.title: Ski Sunday
programme.desc: All the news, gossip and action from the piste(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels the length of California. He climbs to the top of one of the world's tallest trees, and meets the fire crews tasked with tackling the ever-present danger of wildfires, a growing threat given the state's changing climate and chronic shortage of water. He also meets street doctors providing much-needed medical support to people living in extreme poverty and visits a city on the desert for Americans who have dropped out of the rat race entirely(n)

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams have reached the halfway point of their journey. For the next leg, must choose whether to travel through Bolivia or Chile - both countries with unstable political climates. Sam and Jo decide to break up their travel with regular breaks, while Jen and Rob have an explosive argument when they get caught up in Bolivia's general election. Emon and Jamiul find work in an Argentinian salt mine, and Dom has a health scare that may require him to pull out of the race(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones consider three more money-making proposals. The ideas under deliberation are an environmentally friendly range of plastic-free water bottles made from starch, ergonomic underwear, and an armchair tray for elderly people. Evan Davis presents(n)

programme.title: Trigonometry
programme.desc: Kieran and Gemma go on honeymoon, but cannot stop thinking about Ray and what they may have left behind - leaving them faced with a big decision about their future. Back home, Ray makes a disastrous attempt to reconnect with Moira and starts a new job. She is soon thriving on being part of a team again, but may not have space in her life for Kieran and Gemma when they return(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Tom Allen guest stars on the topical comedy show hosted by Romesh Ranganathan, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today(n)

programme.title: Kon-Tiki (2012)
programme.desc: An anthropologist forms a theory that Polynesia was settled by explorers from South America prior to Christopher Columbus' expedition. To prove his claims are possible, he and a small crew sail a wooden raft across the Pacific using no modern technology. Fact-based drama, starring Pal Sverre Hagen and Anders Baasmo Christiansen. In Norwegian, English, French and Swedish(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce chairs the topical debate, where the panel discuss the coronavirus and the implications that are affecting the UK and the rest of the world(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on a millionaire who let the public fund his car racing hobby, and the boss of a recycling firm who helped himself to millions in government money(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Max anxiously awaits a reunion with her estranged daughter, but Ric and Ange are concerned that she will not be able to juggle her work commitments with this important encounter. A new nurse joins Darwin, but Jac questions whether he has the specialist skills needed to carry out the role. Essie waits to discover whether her cancer has returned, while Sacha lets Dom in on a secret - he plans to propose(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion present from the Shropshire town of Oswestry, where two teams shop for antiques with the help of experts Kate Bliss and Ochuko Ojiri. Christina also visits one of the most historically important buildings in Britain, a flax mill in Shrewsbury that can lay claim to being the world's first skyscraper(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final, which sees the last three contestants rising to the challenge. To shake things up, however, the judges present their tasks in a different order to normal. Chris Bavin's 'rustle up' comes first, after which it's on to another of Angela Hartnett's tricky recipes in the eliminator. Sadly, one of the hopefuls will then receive their marching orders, leaving the other two to take on Mary Berry's challenge - a full three-course meal which will secure one of them the title(n)

programme.title: The Sweet Makers
programme.desc: Four modern confectioners relive the world of historical sweets, making treats that have not been tasted in centuries from original recipes. The first episode focuses on the Tudor era, where the team makes candied roses and roots, sugar plates and goblets and a model banqueting house, while historians Annie Gray and Emma Dabiri reveal how sweets were a sign of wealth, but were made possible through the slave trade(n)

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as two teams put their knowledge of antiques to the test, while competing to build the most valuable collection for a chance to win £1,000. In the first edition, Lynda and Kate from Bath take on Alice and James from the south coast of England as they go head to head to determine who will meet today's curious collector Sue(n)

programme.title: The Dark: Nature's Nighttime World
programme.desc: Biologists and camera crews explore the wildernesses of South and Central America at night, discovering how wildlife changes when skies get dark. They begin with an expedition to the jungles of Costa Rica, where they encounter some of the world's deadliest nocturnal predators. Entomologist George McGavin searches for the net-casting spider, which has a distinctive method of catching its prey, while camera operator Justine Evans gets perilously close to a male jaguar and Gordon Buchanan discovers the remains of ruined temples(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Sylvia Syms narrates a selection of interviews with Oscar-winnning actress Jane Fonda, from her successful - if sometimes controversial - career in Hollywood(n)

programme.title: School for Scoundrels (1960)
programme.desc: A timid company owner is pushed around by his employees, deceived by a car salesman, humiliated by a waiter and finally loses his girlfriend to a dastardly rival. Salvation comes in the form of an unusual college dedicated to teaching students how to succeed through being manipulative and devious, and he sets about getting even with his enemies. Comedy, starring Ian Carmichael, Terry-Thomas, Alastair Sim and Janette Scott(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Adam Partridge search for items to sell at auction at Penrhyn Castle in north Wales. Paul Martin uncovers the story of Britain's longest-running industrial strike(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu, Charlie Higson, Kate Williams and Tom Allen go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Jean Johansson finds out how Longleat's giant otters Gordito and Inny are getting on, while Kate Humble helps put the European grey wolves' stamina to the test(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers discover the culinary delights of Corsica, starting out in a delicatessen to get a taste of the local cuisine, including the figatelli sausage. The owner explains how most of the island's food is made up of a holy trinity of ingredients - meat, cheese and chestnuts, a list that forms the basis of the bikers' exploration(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys hosts the third semi-final of the quiz, inviting four returning contenders to brave the black chair once more. They answer questions on the specialist subjects of queens consort of England from 939 to 1154, Flight of the Conchords, Richard Wagner, and Caravaggio, before they each have two minutes to demonstrate their general knowledge(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The first of the semi-finals sees two teams of four students battling it out to make it to the climax of the competition and the chance to take home the trophy. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Terror in Paradise
programme.desc: Last Easter eight men triggered huge bombs in churches and tourist hotels across Sri Lanka, killing more than 270 people in one of the worst Islamist terror attacks since 9/11. Jane Corbin talks to survivors and those who came face to face with the killers. Drawing on hours of CCTV footage that track the bombers every move, the film pieces together how the attacks were carried out. Part of the This World strand(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Maisie Adam, Ed Byrne, Sophie Duker, Ed Gamble and Mark Simmons and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: The Mini Mash Report
programme.desc: Highlights from the satirical news show, presented by Nish Kumar, as a cast of comedy correspondents give their take on the week's events(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Cops Like Us
programme.desc: The Staffordshire Police officers look to the future as they take on new recruits, including trainee Holly Swift. After working in mental health services and seeing the difference police officers can make, she decided it was the career for her. At the other end of the career path is Sgt Dave Allan - who is just about to complete 30 years' service and retire. The police service recruited heavily after the miners' strikes in the 1980s, and many of those officers are now approaching retirement, including Dave(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Palermo in northern Sicily, a city echoing Arab, North African, French, Spanish and Italian influences, making its cuisine unique and irresistible. Pine nuts, raisins, almonds and golden breadcrumbs are combined with flavours of sun-ripened tomatoes, lemons and the finest olive oil to create a range of unforgettable dishes. Rick witnesses locals celebrating a festival in honour of Palermo's patron saint Rosalia, and receives a cookery lesson from a real-life duchess. At home he cooks Marsala chicken, a Sicilian classic served in UK bistros since the 1960s(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver and Tessa Dunlop continue their comprehensive guide to Britain's coastline, this time turning attention to the North Sea coast, a stretch that takes in two nations, and is bordered by a pair of huge estuaries, the Forth in Scotland, and the Humber in England. Neil sheds light on a military shipbuilding project in Rosyth, and heads to the island of Inchmickery to investigate a bird colony with a difference. From there, he tells a tragic story that still haunts the fishing village of Eyemouth, and then visits the holy island of Lindisfarne to make a dramatic archeological discovery(n)

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: Erik lives alone in a run-down campervan on a desolate beach, while his twin Adam is the complete opposite - a successful man with money, a thriving family business, a loving wife and two children. When Erik's irresponsible lifestyle finally catches up with him, he contacts his brother for help, but Adam brutally rejects him - with fatal consequences. Norwegian crime thriller, starring Kristofer Hivju(n)

programme.title: Twin
programme.desc: As she takes in what she has done, Ingrid realises that she risks losing both home and family. In desperation, she begins to lie about the tragedy, and when the police show up, she quickly winds up in a web of deceit. Norwegian crime thriller, starring Kristofer Hivju and Rebekka Nystabakk(n)

programme.title: Twin Sisters: A World Apart
programme.desc: The story of identical twin girls Alexandra and Mia, who were adopted separately from China, with one growing up in California, and the other in Sweden. A chance sighting at the orphanage prompted the adoptive parents to suspect the girls were related, and a DNA test proved their theory. The film follows the children as they meet in Norway aged eight years old, and captures the similarities between the pair, despite having grown up thousands of miles apart(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Simon Parkin host the edition first shown on June 15, featuring performances by Fuzzbox, REM, Sinitta, Double Trouble & Rebel MC, Donna Allen, Cyndi Lauper, Jason Donovan and Tom Petty(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Gary Davies host the edition first shown on June 22, featuring performances by Living in a Box, the Beautiful South, Gladys Knight, Clannad & Bono, Placido Domingo & Jennifer Rush, Mob featuring LRS, Soul II Soul and the Bangles(n)

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: Singer, DJ and broadcaster Cerys Matthews joins acclaimed blues photographer Val Wilmer to select their favourite blues musicians - several of whom Val has met and photographed. From Muddy Waters and Howlin' Wolf to Jimi Hendrix and John Lee Hooker, Sister Rosetta Tharpe to Peggy Lee and many more, their playlist is packed with classic blues and punctuated with personal stories(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver and Tessa Dunlop continue their comprehensive guide to Britain's coastline, this time turning attention to the North Sea coast, a stretch that takes in two nations, and is bordered by a pair of huge estuaries, the Forth in Scotland, and the Humber in England. Neil sheds light on a military shipbuilding project in Rosyth, and heads to the island of Inchmickery to investigate a bird colony with a difference. From there, he tells a tragic story that still haunts the fishing village of Eyemouth, and then visits the holy island of Lindisfarne to make a dramatic archeological discovery(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Palermo in northern Sicily, a city echoing Arab, North African, French, Spanish and Italian influences, making its cuisine unique and irresistible. Pine nuts, raisins, almonds and golden breadcrumbs are combined with flavours of sun-ripened tomatoes, lemons and the finest olive oil to create a range of unforgettable dishes. Rick witnesses locals celebrating a festival in honour of Palermo's patron saint Rosalia, and receives a cookery lesson from a real-life duchess. At home he cooks Marsala chicken, a Sicilian classic served in UK bistros since the 1960s(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Ice
programme.desc: Science writer Gabrielle Walker explores the unique properties of ice, investigating the seemingly contradictory qualities that enable it to shatter rocks and yet melt away almost instantaneously. She analyses the structure of ice crystals and reveals the substance's crucial role in forming Earth's landscape and preserving its history(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary telling how the world's smallest animals manage to survive, revealing how they have evolved extraordinary skills and achieved mindboggling feats. From the animal kingdom's greatest artist to the tiny creatures that provide people with so much of the air they breathe, the programme discovers what it takes to be a miniature miracle. Hugh Dennis narrates(n)

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Felicity Aston and Dr Kevin Fong finally arrive at the end of their gruelling journey in Dawson City. They set up a traditional mining camp in the heart of the gold fields, before facing their next challenge. They have just five days to find gold in an area that has already been heavily mined, and can only use the back-breaking methods of the 19th-century early pioneers(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Severely facially disfigured broadcaster Adam Pearson presents a personal film about genetics, asking why the same genetic disease, neurofibromatosis 1, can affect he and his twin brother Neil so differently. For Adam, the disease has left tumours on his face that are growing out of control and he could lose his sight. For years, everyone thought his brother had escaped symptoms, but today his life is governed by epilepsy and a mysterious memory loss, that suddenly came on during his teens(n)

programme.title: Bee Gees at the BBC and Beyond
programme.desc: A compilation of classic performances by the legendary pop trio from the BBC archive. Including renditions of the brothers' most popular hits, 1960s appearances on European television performing I Started a Joke, footage from a Top of the Pops staging of World, and some later concerts from the 1990s(n)

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed in real time and without voiceover or music, this series examines the crafting of handmade objects. The first programme observes expert Michael Ruh carrying out the time-consuming process of blowing and rolling glass to make a simple jug. Glass is heated in a steel crucible until it becomes liquid. Ruh's task is to keep a globule hot and in constant motion as he shapes it(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez sets ex-forger Shaun Greenhalgh the challenge of making an alabaster carving of the kind mass-produced in Catholic England in the centuries before the Reformation(n)

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Tom Service presents a detailed analysis of Finnish composer Kaija Saariaho's 1994 violin concerto Graal Théâtre, followed by a performance of the work by the BBC Philharmonic Orchestra conducted by Ludovic Morlot. Tom talks to members of the orchestra and soloist Peter Herresthal about the challenges of playing this demanding piece of music(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary telling how the world's smallest animals manage to survive, revealing how they have evolved extraordinary skills and achieved mindboggling feats. From the animal kingdom's greatest artist to the tiny creatures that provide people with so much of the air they breathe, the programme discovers what it takes to be a miniature miracle. Hugh Dennis narrates(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Severely facially disfigured broadcaster Adam Pearson presents a personal film about genetics, asking why the same genetic disease, neurofibromatosis 1, can affect he and his twin brother Neil so differently. For Adam, the disease has left tumours on his face that are growing out of control and he could lose his sight. For years, everyone thought his brother had escaped symptoms, but today his life is governed by epilepsy and a mysterious memory loss, that suddenly came on during his teens(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Mediterranean Voyage
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto reaches the former Turkish capital, Istanbul, a city founded as the centrepiece of the former Ottoman Empire, bestriding the Bosphorus Strait. After touring the Topkapi Palace and an underground city, he tries selling rugs at the bazaar before taking a relaxing Turkish bath and massage(n)

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of a tiger in the dense jungles of India's Bandhavgarh Reserve. The big cat has four newborn cubs to protect and is the dominant presence in a prime hunting territory. However, rival tigers are determined to take over, and one of her own previous offspring becomes one of the most persistent enemies the mother and her cubs face(n)

programme.title: Du Fu: China's Greatest Poet
programme.desc: Michael Wood travels in the footsteps of the eighth-century poet, from the Yellow River to the Yangtze Gorges and down to the forested hills of Hunan. Born in 712, the age of Beowulf in Britain, Du Fu lived through the violent fall of China's Tang dynasty and as rebel armies sacked the capital and floods and famine wrecked the country, he was forced to flee, taking his family on the roads as refugees. With readings by Ian McKellen(n)

programme.title: Yellow Is Forbidden
programme.desc: Documentary following Chinese fashion designer Guo Pei as she aims to switch from dressing her country's elite to trying to conquer the world of haute couture in Paris(n)

programme.title: Horse People with Alexandra Tolstoy
programme.desc: Equestrian and adventurer Alexandra Tolstoy embarks on a globe-trotting quest to explore a variety of worldwide cultures, all of which rely on relationships with horses to survive in the unique climates of their homelands. This first edition sees Alexandra brave the icy tundras of Siberia, where she encounters Yakut horses specially bred to resist the extreme cold(n)

programme.title: Handmade
programme.desc: Filmed over five days, this programme examines the complex and time-consuming process involved in building a classic Windsor chair, made by craftsman Jim Steele in his Warwickshire workshop. The traditional design features woods chosen for their different qualities - ash, elm and yew - and the finished object has just two screws and no glue(n)

programme.title: Source Code (2011)
programme.desc: A military pilot is the subject of a top-secret experiment that transfers his mind into the body of a man killed in a terrorist bombing on a train, but only for the last eight minutes of the dead man's life. His mission is to use that time to find out who is responsible and if they intend to strike again. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Michelle Monaghan, Vera Farmiga and Jeffrey Wright(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Tina Daheley and Ben Thompson present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by Arwyn Jones(n)

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: As Holy Week begins, Dean of Hereford the Very Rev Michael Tavinor leads a service for Palm Sunday from the city's historic cathedral. The programme features hymns recorded for Songs of Praise in 2017(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey help two teams find items of value at Harrogate antiques fair, scouring the event in the hope that they will make a profit at auction. Amidst the highs and lows in the saleroom, presenter Anita Manning manages to find time to also embark on a quilting quest at a local museum(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: For Palm Sunday, Sean Fletcher visits Glasgow Cathedral to discuss Holy Week and hears how this historic building plays a prominent role in the Christian life of the city(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson goes country property shopping in Hampshire with a buyer who has an £875,000 budget and a firm wish to return to her family roots - which is why she has brought her cousin along for support. Away from the house hunt, Jules visits Mottisfont Abbey and is treated to a tour of the historic rose garden, home to a collection of over 700 different varieties, some of which are rare and centuries old(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne visits Working Recycling Centre, rescuing a leather armchair for bag-maker Neil Wragg to get his hands on and an old water pump, which he gives to lighting expert Guy Trench. For his own project, he tries to repurpose a collection of old wooden boxes(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are in Southsea on the South Coast, helping mum Christine and daughter Tracey, whose garden has been affected by building work(n)

programme.title: Only Foals and Horses
programme.desc: The daily lives of two horse vets working from their clinic in Carmarthenshire. Lisa Durham and Philippa Hughes face an uncooperative wild Welsh mountain pony stallion who needs sedating before an operation, an elderly horse with a wobbly tooth and a champion mare with a badly gashed leg facing emergency treatment(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: On a route from Pwll to Kidwelly, Derek Brockway turns Pied Piper as he launches a walk of 800 children marking the anniversary of 'People Power' that saved a stretch of the Loughor Estuary for families to enjoy. On this Carmarthenshire coastal walk, the weatherman also discovers a former munitions factory that employed 6,000 people, has a spin in a racing car and learns the story Wales' feisty warrior princess Gwenllian(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet Revisited
programme.desc: Scientists find out what the future holds for the Great Barrier Reef, monitoring green turtles, following families of breeding birds and meeting the reef's curious residents(n)

programme.title: The Snow Spider
programme.desc: Gwyn tries to find out the identity of the pale girl in Arianwen's web, while his magical powers are putting a strain on his relationships with best friend Alun and his parents. Fantasy drama based on Jenny Nimmo's trilogy, starring Fflyn Edwards(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: It is all systems go in the lambing shed as Adam Henson and Helen Skelton work round the clock to help a Wirral farmer at one of his busiest times of the year. Plus, Sean Fletcher is in Carmarthenshire to learn first-hand about the rigours of lambing outdoors.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return for a second visit to Lytham Hall in Lancashire, where items assessed include a chair believed to be from Nelson's flagship and a series of watercolours rescued from a skip and worth thousands. Geoffrey Munn finds a diamond pendant presented by the city of Liverpool, and there is some 1960s rock 'n roll memorabilia of the highest pedigree(n)

programme.title: The Nest
programme.desc: It has been weeks since Dan learnt the truth about Kaya's past and he is tormented by the dilemma of whether to tell Emily, who has built a firm friendship with the teenager and is finally allowing herself to believe that there will be a baby at the end of all this. Kaya and Jack start to dream about what their lives will be like after the baby is born, but on the night of their school prom Hilary makes a decision that has devastating repercussions for everyone. Thriller, starring Martin Compston, Sophie Rundle and Mirren Mack(n)

programme.title: Critical: Inside Intensive Care
programme.desc: Documentary following staff and patients at Newport's Royal Gwent Hospital, focusing on the ICU as medics treat people whose lives hang in the balance. In the first edition, the team treat several patients whose hearts have stopped(n)

programme.title: A Monster Calls (2016)
programme.desc: Young Conor struggles to deal with his mother's terminal illness, the cold grandmother he has to stay with and the bullying he receives at school. He is woken from a recurring nightmare by the visitation of a tree monster at his bedroom window, telling him a series of stories. In return, Conor must tell the monster the truth behind his dreadful nightmare. Fantasy drama based on the award-winning book by Patrick Ness, starring Lewis MacDougall, Felicity Jones, Sigourney Weaver and Toby Kebbell, with the voice of Liam Neeson(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: HealthCheck UK Live
programme.desc: Daily advice for viewers in self-isolation due to the Covid-19 outbreak(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a Birmingham property with a tiny kitchen, a former care home near Conwy and a house in Merseyside(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help teams to buy items to make a profit at auction. Natasha also visits the National Tramway Museum in Crich to learn about the role trams played in delivering the nation's letters(n)

BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Izzie arrives ahead of schedule, putting Daniel's plans in disarray. Zara goes to see Emma, while Jasmine gets irritated at work, and Ruhma finds herself caught in the middle(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man is murdered at the unveiling of a new church clock and the cleric must act quickly before an innocent woman is sent to the gallows. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton leads a couple in their search for a countryside home in the border between Staffordshire and Derbyshire border on a budget of £320,000. Later, Alistair puts his tailoring skills to the test when he visits the manufacturing factory of heritage knitwear brand John Smedley(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling expert EJ Osborne is at Woking Recycling Centre searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp, including an old bedroom cabinet and a box of wooden blocks(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers who toil through the small hours to get the world ready for when the rest of the population awakes. In the first edition, vets perform potentially life-saving surgery on a much-loved horse, and bus cleaners work to have Newcastle's transport ready for commuters(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, restoring a set of grocer's scales, a leather keepsake bag and an ornamental Egyptian table(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: More than one and a half million people have been told they have to stay at home for the next three months at least because they are most at risk from coronavirus, with the Government promising to do whatever it takes to support them. Richard Bilton hears from some of the most vulnerable about how their lives have changed and the people trying to help them(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian takes great pleasure in kicking Dotty out but he may regret his actions when she realises what he has done and plans her next move. A drunken Billy helps Linda and Mick put their problems into perspective, Chantelle throws herself into work with the Panesars, and Bobby develops a crush on Dotty(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: An investigation into an alternative therapist who says he has a machine that can treat cancer, while Rachel Treadaway-Williams exposes the trade in fake memory sticks(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals begin with a spectacular challenge as the nine remaining contestants head to the Royal Navy base in Portsmouth, where they have to cook for HMS Diamond's Trafalgar Night celebration dinner. Split into three teams, they face the enormous challenge of mass catering at fine-dining level from the ship's galley. They then return to the MasterChef kitchen, where they must create a dish to hold onto their hard-fought place in the competition, with two being sent home at the end by judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace(n)

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: While trying to do a good deed by helping out at the local food bank, Mobeen is also under strict orders from Uncle Khan to launder money - and juggling the two things could leave him with a deadly debt that's impossible to pay(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with actor and comedian Miles Jupp and journalist Helen Lewis joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: World darts champion Peter Wright, Paralympic great Sarah Storey, former Premier League striker Dion Dublin and ex-England cricketer Steve Harmison join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Anna Haugh, and special guest Tom Allen joins via video link. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Mary Berry(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Family Favourites
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain demonstrates quick recipes for days when everything is a bit hectic, including mango and lime lassi bircher, a spin on prawn toast, seafood crumble, and a simple apple Rocky Road recipe. Nadiya's love for the simplicity of cereal takes her to the world's first cereal café in London, and her appreciation for bread sees her meet passionate baker Heather and the members of her club(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a round-up of the best football has to offer, featuring Sheffield United manager Chris Wilder and Chelsea and England star Callum Hudson-Odoi(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings(n)

programme.title: Rugby League: Challenge Cup Classics
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of five memorable finals - Leeds v St Helens from 1978, Wigan v Hull FC from 1985, St Helens v Bradford Bulls from 1996, Bradford Bulls v Leeds Rhinos from 2003 and Hull FC v Warrington Wolves from 2016(n)

programme.title: World Cup Rewind
programme.desc: Guy Mowbray introduces highlights from five classic matches from World Cups down the years, including Scotland v Netherlands from 1978 and USA v Portugal from 2002(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is in Surrey, checking out the cast-offs at Witley Recycling Centre, where she comes across a drum kit to repurpose(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an expat couple who want to return from Cyprus to buy a house in Lincolnshire, and also visits a college to find out about the next generation of farmers(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight Paralympic alpine skier Kelly Gallagher, comedian and presenter Stephen Bailey, actor and comedy writer Daniel Lawrence Taylor, and presenter and mathematician Bobby Seagull(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Marty finally acknowledges how dangerous his behaviour has been outside of work and decides to change his ways. Meanwhile, Ethan questions what he really wants when Fenisha turns him down, and David suggests that Dylan's behaviour indicates feelings towards Faith, but Dylan denies it. Starring Shaheen Jafargholi and George Rainsford(n)

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy about the romance between a couple with differing cultures and backgrounds. Gavin and Stacey have flirted for months on the phone at work but have never met up - until she travels from Wales to London to see him. Mathew Horne and Joanna Page star alongside Rob Brydon, Alison Steadman, Ruth Jones and James Corden(n)

programme.title: Outnumbered
programme.desc: The Brockmans have to make some tough decisions about work and video games, as well as who to take to a family funeral - an event that has the vicar wishing none of them had ever come. Comedy, starring Hugh Dennis, Claire Skinner and Tyger Drew-Honey(n)

programme.title: All Round to Mrs Brown's
programme.desc: Mrs Brown's Boys creator, writer and star Brendan O'Carroll dons Agnes's famous cardigan and curly wig once again as he throws open the doors of the larger-than-life Irish matriarch's home to host the Saturday night chat show. In the third episode of the series, Agnes and the family are joined on the show by Spice Girl and girl power icon Mel B, comedy actor and Little Britain star Matt Lucas, with his mother Diana Lobatto. Plus, a special musical performance from Jax Jones and Raye(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright for a discussion on the top 10 goalkeepers in Premier League history, including Peter Schmeichel(n)

programme.title: Match of Their Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights from three classic Premier League matches, as chosen by some of the regular expert analysts that appear on the show. Plus, a special musical performance from Jax Jones and Raye(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Ian Wright for a discussion on the top 10 goalkeepers in Premier League history, including Peter Schmeichel(n)

programme.title: Match of Their Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights from three classic Premier League matches, as chosen by some of the regular expert analysts that appear on the show. This week's selection includes the memorable encounter from April 1996 between Liverpool and Newcastle United at Anfield, as chosen by Alan Shearer(n)

programme.title: Source Code (2011)
programme.desc: A military pilot is the subject of a top-secret experiment that transfers his mind into the body of a man killed in a terrorist bombing on a train, but only for the last eight minutes of the dead man's life. His mission is to use that time to find out who is responsible and if they intend to strike again. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Michelle Monaghan, Vera Farmiga and Jeffrey Wright(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Tina Daheley and Ben Thompson present a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: The programme follows David Boyd, a successful businessman hoping to return Portavogie to the 'good old days' of the 1970s and 80s white-fish boom, by developing new markets for the large coldwater prawn or langoustine, a high-value luxury food. David buys new boats and tries to encourage youngsters to stay in the area and join the fishing fleet(n)

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: As Holy Week begins, Dean of Hereford the Very Rev Michael Tavinor leads a service for Palm Sunday from the city's historic cathedral. The programme features hymns recorded for Songs of Praise in 2017(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned, then talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Mark Stacey help two teams find items of value at Harrogate antiques fair, scouring the event in the hope that they will make a profit at auction. Amidst the highs and lows in the saleroom, presenter Anita Manning manages to find time to also embark on a quilting quest at a local museum(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: For Palm Sunday, Sean Fletcher visits Glasgow Cathedral to discuss Holy Week and hears how this historic building plays a prominent role in the Christian life of the city(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson goes country property shopping in Hampshire with a buyer who has an £875,000 budget and a firm wish to return to her family roots - which is why she has brought her cousin along for support. Away from the house hunt, Jules visits Mottisfont Abbey and is treated to a tour of the historic rose garden, home to a collection of over 700 different varieties, some of which are rare and centuries old(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: EJ Osborne visits Working Recycling Centre, rescuing a leather armchair for bag-maker Neil Wragg to get his hands on and an old water pump, which he gives to lighting expert Guy Trench. For his own project, he tries to repurpose a collection of old wooden boxes(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich are in Southsea on the South Coast, helping mum Christine and daughter Tracey, whose garden has been affected by building work(n)

programme.title: The Croods (2013)
programme.desc: Prehistoric family the Croods find themselves with nowhere to live when their cave is destroyed. They embark on a dangerous trek through an unstable landscape overrun with all manner of dangerous predators in search of a new home, but their domestic squabbles prove as much of an obstacle as the wildlife. Animated comedy, with the voices of Nicolas Cage and Ryan Reynolds(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet Revisited
programme.desc: Scientists find out what the future holds for the Great Barrier Reef, monitoring green turtles, following families of breeding birds and meeting the reef's curious residents(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: It is all systems go in the lambing shed as Adam Henson and Helen Skelton work round the clock to help a Wirral farmer at one of his busiest times of the year. Plus, Sean Fletcher is in Carmarthenshire to learn first-hand about the rigours of lambing outdoors.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return for a second visit to Lytham Hall in Lancashire, where items assessed include a chair believed to be from Nelson's flagship and a series of watercolours rescued from a skip and worth thousands. Geoffrey Munn finds a diamond pendant presented by the city of Liverpool, and there is some 1960s rock 'n roll memorabilia of the highest pedigree(n)

programme.title: The Nest
programme.desc: It has been weeks since Dan learnt the truth about Kaya's past and he is tormented by the dilemma of whether to tell Emily, who has built a firm friendship with the teenager and is finally allowing herself to believe that there will be a baby at the end of all this. Kaya and Jack start to dream about what their lives will be like after the baby is born, but on the night of their school prom Hilary makes a decision that has devastating repercussions for everyone. Thriller, starring Martin Compston, Sophie Rundle and Mirren Mack(n)

programme.title: A Monster Calls (2016)
programme.desc: Young Conor struggles to deal with his mother's terminal illness, the cold grandmother he has to stay with and the bullying he receives at school. He is woken from a recurring nightmare by the visitation of a tree monster at his bedroom window, telling him a series of stories. In return, Conor must tell the monster the truth behind his dreadful nightmare. Fantasy drama based on the award-winning book by Patrick Ness, starring Lewis MacDougall, Felicity Jones, Sigourney Weaver and Toby Kebbell, with the voice of Liam Neeson(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: HealthCheck UK Live
programme.desc: Daily advice for viewers in self-isolation due to the Covid-19 outbreak(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a Birmingham property with a tiny kitchen, a former care home near Conwy and a house in Merseyside(n)

programme.title: Night Force
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of the army of night workers who toil through the small hours to get the world ready for when the rest of the population awakes. In the first edition, vets perform potentially life-saving surgery on a much-loved horse, and bus cleaners work to have Newcastle's transport ready for commuters(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp hosts from Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, where experts Thomas Forrester and Tim Weeks help teams to buy items to make a profit at auction. Natasha also visits the National Tramway Museum in Crich to learn about the role trams played in delivering the nation's letters(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Izzie arrives ahead of schedule, putting Daniel's plans in disarray. Zara goes to see Emma, while Jasmine gets irritated at work, and Ruhma finds herself caught in the middle(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A man is murdered at the unveiling of a new church clock and the cleric must act quickly before an innocent woman is sent to the gallows. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton leads a couple in their search for a countryside home in the border between Staffordshire and Derbyshire border on a budget of £320,000. Later, Alistair puts his tailoring skills to the test when he visits the manufacturing factory of heritage knitwear brand John Smedley(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling expert EJ Osborne is at Woking Recycling Centre searching for three items that he can rescue and revamp, including an old bedroom cabinet and a box of wooden blocks(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms back to life, restoring a set of grocer's scales, a leather keepsake bag and an ornamental Egyptian table(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: More than one and a half million people have been told they have to stay at home for the next three months at least because they are most at risk from coronavirus, with the Government promising to do whatever it takes to support them. Richard Bilton hears from some of the most vulnerable about how their lives have changed and the people trying to help them(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian takes great pleasure in kicking Dotty out but he may regret his actions when she realises what he has done and plans her next move. A drunken Billy helps Linda and Mick put their problems into perspective, Chantelle throws herself into work with the Panesars, and Bobby develops a crush on Dotty(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian Jo Brand, singer Emma Bunton, athlete Jonnie Peacock and actor Shaun Williamson join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The semi-finals begin with a spectacular challenge as the nine remaining contestants head to the Royal Navy base in Portsmouth, where they have to cook for HMS Diamond's Trafalgar Night celebration dinner. Split into three teams, they face the enormous challenge of mass catering at fine-dining level from the ship's galley. They then return to the MasterChef kitchen, where they must create a dish to hold onto their hard-fought place in the competition, with two being sent home at the end by judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace(n)

programme.title: My First Home
programme.desc: Vinny Hurrell presents the programme following people who are attempting to get on the property ladder, with nterior designer Ian Thompson joining the potential buyers to visit three properties and a dream house for inspiration. Returning to the studio, a virtual makeover of their chosen house is revealed, to give them an idea of what can be done for their budget(n)

programme.title: Man Like Mobeen
programme.desc: While trying to do a good deed by helping out at the local food bank, Mobeen is also under strict orders from Uncle Khan to launder money - and juggling the two things could leave him with a deadly debt that's impossible to pay(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with actor and comedian Miles Jupp and journalist Helen Lewis joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events(n)

programme.desc: The House of Lords Democracy and Digital Technology select committee session with evidence from Vint Cerf, chief internet evangelist and vice-president at Google and Katie O'Donovan, head of UK government affairs and public policy at Google, from Monday 9 March.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Huw, Sudeh, Thomas and Nadia pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: The Virtual Grand National
programme.desc: Nick Luck and guests watch as Red Rum takes on Tiger Roll in a special race and they reveal how the 2020 Grand National may have been won(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four celebrity contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Emmerdale's Michael Parr (Ross Barton), England footballer Alex Scott, Hollyoaks actress Jennifer Metcalfe (Mercedes McQueen) and TV presenter Bill Turnbull must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo's entertainment show, featuring undercover pranks, surprises galore, star guests and a chance for a member of the audience to win the items advertised in one of last week's commercial breaks. Following the coronavirus outbreak, the planned series finale at Walt Disney World in Florida has been cancelled. However, the Geordie pair will surely have something up their sleeves to put a smile on the faces of the viewing public(n)

programme.title: In for a Penny

programme.title: The Epic Tales of Captain Underpants
